topic.what: Achieving single online round threshold ML-DSA signatures in the TSaaS (Threshold Signatures as-a-Service) model where a Client requests signatures from remote parties
topic.how: Combines first two rounds into one message-independent preprocessing round using a 256-bit random salt that prevents ROS-style attacks; leverages Client/Relayer architecture for unpredictability
topic.why_matters: Enables practical post-quantum threshold signatures for Wallet-as-a-Service with 2-3x speedup and reduced bandwidth over prior work while maintaining NIST FIPS 204 compliance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time for online signing phase (single signing round plus combine)
characteristics.vs_baseline: 2-3x faster than [Celi et al., USENIX 26] (771ms for (2,3))
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 averages include rejection sampling aborts
characteristics.context: ML-DSaaSsh with N≤6; latency scales with threshold and repetition parameter K

characteristics.what_itasures: Network bandwidth during online signing phase
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 conservative upper bounds via libp2p-pubsub
characteristics.context: WAN deployment across AWS regions with ≥290ms RTT floor

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of rounds requiring message to be known
characteristics.context: Key optimization: preprocessing before message known enables single online round

characteristics.what_it_measures: Probability protocol produces valid signature (accounts for rejection sampling aborts)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Identical to [Celi et al.] by Proposition 1
characteristics.context: Depends on rejection sampling parameters and lattice parameters (ML-DSA-44)

characteristics.vs_baseline: Slightly smaller advantage than [Celi et al.]
characteristics.context: Under MLWE, ROM; advantage bound includes Qs terms for signing queries
Constrained by ML-DSA-44 parameters and NIST compliance
